pattern: Sort, then scan: when a value is not greater than the previous assigned value, lift it to exactly one above the previous, accumulating the difference as the move cost.
hints: Sort the array. After sorting, duplicates are adjacent and you can decide each value's final position in one left-to-right pass.
Maintain the minimum allowed value for each position. If nums[i] is already above the previous minimum, keep it; otherwise raise it to the minimum and add the difference to your total moves.
Update the minimum for the next position after each element. No element ever needs to decrease, so the greedy one-pass answer is optimal.

edge_inputs.description: all same values, must spread them out
already all unique, zero moves needed
already unique but unsorted — sort first

stress.description: 100k random values from 0..100000, expects heavy duplicates
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
